description:
You are a DevSecOps engineer who must quickly enforce a “ports-must-be-well-known” policy on a CSV inventory.  
Everything you need is already on disk.

1. A comma-separated file exists at  
   /home/user/policies/service_ports.csv

2. Your job is to create BOTH of the following artefacts inside the directory  
   /home/user/output  
   (create the directory first if it does not yet exist):
   a. /home/user/output/high_ports.txt  
      • Contains ONLY the TCP port numbers that are **strictly greater than 1024**.  
      • One port per line, nothing else, no extra spaces or blank lines.  
      • The list must be numerically sorted in ascending order and deduplicated.  
   b. /home/user/output/compliance.log  
      • Single-line log entry in the exact form  
        High ports extracted: N  
        where N is the total count of lines (ports) that ended up in high_ports.txt.

3. Use awk, sed, or a single Unix text-processing pipeline (no external scripting languages) to produce both files in one command if possible.  

4. After running your command(s), verify your work by printing the contents of both new files to the terminal so the auditor can see the results.

The automated tests will check:
• The presence of /home/user/output/high_ports.txt and /home/user/output/compliance.log  
• That high_ports.txt contains only the correct port numbers, one per line, sorted ascending, no duplicates.  
• That compliance.log contains exactly one line matching the required format with the correct count.
truth:
Initial file present before task execution:
/home/user/policies/service_ports.csv
------------------------------------------------
service,port
ssh,22
http,80
https,443
custom-app,8080
db-mysql,3306
db-postgres,5432
old-telnet,23
custom-admin,10022
dev-service,12000
------------------------------------------------

Expected artefacts after successful completion:

1. /home/user/output/high_ports.txt  
   Exact byte-for-byte contents (including trailing newline):  
------------------------------------------------
3306
5432
8080
10022
12000
------------------------------------------------

2. /home/user/output/compliance.log  
   Exact byte-for-byte contents (including trailing newline):  
------------------------------------------------
High ports extracted: 5
------------------------------------------------
name: task_000000_9a7c9917
